Layer diagnostic: the evolved jr edge error is 5e-9 -> 1.5e-9 (CONVERGING, ~1st-2nd order) — the field is fine; psi0 is the problem: DECISIVE CONDITIONING ANALYSIS: psi0_wt/J-scale ~ 1e-8 (r^-5 peeling suppression of the outgoing datum — same physics as the iter-17 CCM-vs-Sommerfeld degeneracy); extracting psi0 to 5e-3 rel needs ~1e-11 relative field accuracy; the FD-4 truncation ceiling is ~3.7e-7 (inner) — FOUR orders short at any practical grid. This is structural to FD, and exactly why SpECTRE's CCE is SPECTRAL. (Taylor-stage BC probe also failed, 215/23570 — mis-built k3; moot given the conditioning result.)

change_summary: N14 stage C part 2 first wiring: live worldtube map BCs (anchored gauge) fed to the RINGED spectral solver (cheb_evolve_J_sat_bc)
metric.name: psi0_rel_error_anchored_e2e
expected: anchored BCs evolve like plain BCs (psi0 gauge-invariant, verified symbolically: psi0_from_J(J_ANCH) == psi0_from_J(J_bondi))
observed: tube psi0 rel 1447, probe rel 11306. Root structure: the worldtube-anchored gauge has beta != 0 and NON-DECAYING tails (k=0,1 monomials in J/U/W: J -> a(u) + b(u)/r at scri); the ringed variables jr = r J, ur = r^2 U diverge at scri on such data (1/(1-y) pole, not representable in Chebyshev polynomials), and the beta = 0 Bondi-gauge hierarchy is inconsistent with anchored BC values (Q/W/H equations gain beta sources)
root_cause: ringed regularization assumes Bondi-gauge falloffs; the live-data (anchored) gauge breaks them; additionally the verified hierarchy was beta = 0 only (the documented beta-row caveat coming due)
fix_hypothesis: RESOLVED same iteration: (1) machine-derive the beta-corrected hierarchy on the anchored series (exact-rational fits: Q source -4 r eth(beta); W source -(4/3) ethbar eth beta; H equation jointly refit = (1/2) r dr2 J + dr J + eth2 beta/r - (1/2) eth Q/r - eth U, exact on BOTH families); (2) evolve UNRINGED t-scalars (regular on y in [-1,1] in both gauges, scri rows automatic). Verified: sweeps machine-exact both gauges; anchored end-to-end psi0 probe rel 8.8e-5 (= plain-gauge class), 7.1e-6 at du/2
runtime_metadata.evidence: packages/ZccmJl/test/derive_beta_hierarchy.jl + src/bondisolver_unringed.jl + runtests testsets 'beta-corrected hierarchy' and 'interior-point psi0 + anchored end-to-end'

change_summary: mode-5 live battery first run: Cauchy fields NaN by t=3 (sampled worldtube scalars NaN, solver fed junk, datum feedback blowup)
metric.name: mode5_run_NaN_onset_time
expected: live sampling reproduces the analytic worldtube scalars to Cauchy truncation
observed: t=0 diag already junk in the K-channels (dt_hTT 1.3e-7 = signal scale at a quiescent time; hr 50x signal); NaN in all sampled channels by t=3. Two root causes: (1) the projection read the ADM ALPHA slot of u_adm, but Z4cToADM fills ONLY psi4/g_dd/K_dd - alpha is stale/uninitialized garbage during evolution; (2) off-rank sphere angles carry zero-filled interp values, so gamma=0 aliased to h=-delta in the per-rank sums before MPI_Allreduce (projects to ~0 by shape orthogonality but adds quadrature junk)
root_cause: u_adm gauge slots are not part of the evolution-path ADM update contract; sampling assumed the full enum range was valid
fix_hypothesis: RESOLVED: dt h = -2 K exactly at linear order (alpha = 1 + O(h), correction beyond-linear) - drop alpha from the interpolation range (nvars = I_ADM_KXX+6); skip off-rank angles via the gxx == 0 zero-fill marker (norms stay global; Allreduce assembles the projection). Rerun in flight
